invariant_definition: The first stage of the threefold liberating practice (*śravaṇa-manana-nididhyāsana*) prescribed in *Bṛhadāraṇyaka* 2.4.5 (Yājñavalkya to Maitreyī). *Śravaṇa* is the receptive hearing of *vedānta-vākya* from a qualified *guru*. All Vedāntic schools accept the threefold sequence as the basic structure of *sādhana*; they differ in the relative emphasis (Advaita stresses *śravaṇa* as the proximate cause of *brahma-jñāna*; Viśiṣṭādvaita treats the entire sequence as *upāsana*; Dvaita treats *śravaṇa* as the entry-point to graded *bhakti-jñāna*).

per_school.definition: *Śravaṇa* — receptive hearing of the *vedānta-vākya* through *guru*-mediation — is foregrounded as the proximate occasion for the *mahāvākya*-cognition that produces *akhaṇḍākāra-vṛtti*. *Manana* removes doubts; *nididhyāsana* removes obstructive *pratibandhakas* (residual identification with body-mind).
*Śravaṇa-manana-nididhyāsana* together constitute *upāsana*; the entire sequence is the path, not just *śravaṇa*. Continuity and intensity matter more than any single moment.

translator_note: 'Hearing' captures the literal sense. 'Receptive study' captures the technical sense. The threefold sequence should be preserved as a single technical apparatus.
